retry_reason: The decrement always succeeds; if the refcount is already zero the caller has a double-free bug, so retry is meaningless.
contract_summary: afs_put_symlink releases one reference on an afs_symlink object. When the last reference is dropped (the count reaches zero), the object is freed via kfree_rcu for safe deferred destruction. The caller must not access the object after calling this function. No lock or RCU read-side is required; the RCU free handles memory reclamation. This function is the symmetric release to get/init operations and must be called exactly once for each acquired reference.

judge.reason: The entire function body consists solely of a refcount_decrement-and-test followed by an RCU-free on zero. No other refcount or non-refcount logic exists—it is a pure put wrapper.
